Note: Victoria and Albert Museum photographs are marked with an asterisk.

## Frontispiece

Henry Cole, 1865, photograph by Julia Margaret Cameron.\*

## Plates

I Cole aged 12 in 1820: silhouette by an unknown artist. The Cole family.

2 A Christ's Hospital schoolboy: handcoloured aquatint from R. Ackermann, The History of the Colleges of Winchester, Eton, and Westminster [etc] (London, 1816).\*

3 Blue-coat boys on the sands at Broadstairs: watercolour, from an album of sketches by Henry Cole. V&A: PDP, E.3735-1934 in album 94.F.17.\*

Two pages from Henry Cole's school copy book, 'The Parch'. V&A: NAL, 55.CC.7.\*

5 'My room in 18 Stamford Street Blackfriars about AD 1829' watercolour by Henry Cole, probably dating from 1827. V&A: PDP, E.1-2000.\*

6 John Stuart Mill: photograph of a cameo (apparently now lost) that derives from a portrait (also lost) by a Falmouth artist named Cunningham, made in April 1840. British Library of Political and Economic Science, London School of Economics.

Charles Buller: detail from an engraving from John Saunders, Portraits and memoirs of eminent living political reformers (London: Dowding,1840). National Portrait Gallery.

8 George Grote: detail of a portrait in oil by T. Stewardson, 1824. National Portrait Gallery.

John Arthur Roebuck: detail of a portrait in oil, anonymous and undated. National Portrait Gallery.

1Sir William Molesworth: detail of a photographic reproduction of a sketch by Sir George Hayter (a preliminary study for his oil painting, The House of Commons, 1833), in Millicent Fawcett, Life of the Right Hon. Sir William Molesworth, Bart. (London: Macmillan, 1901).\*

I I Edwin Chadwick: detail of a wood-engraving in Illustrated London News (22 Jan 1848), p.39.\*

12 Henry Cole: drawing by C. C. Pyne, 1830. Private Collection.

I 3 Sir Rowland Hill: wood engraving in Illustrated London News, vol.2 (1843), p. 322.

I4 Design for a postage cover: proof of an engraving on brass by John Thompson after William Mulready. V&A: PDP, E.2977a-1910.

I 5 'Passengers and Luggage being shifted from the Broad Gauge to the Narrow Gauge Carriages': wood engraving after H. J. Townsend in Illustrated London News (6 June 1846), p.369.

16 'His Royal Highness Prince Albert laying the first stone of the docks at Great Grimsby': wood engraving in Illustrated London News (21 April 1849), p.249.

17 Title page and frontispiece of Mrs. Felix Summerly [Marian Cole], The Mother's Primer (London: Longman, 1844). Cambridge University Library.

18 Christmas card designed by J. C. Horsley.\*

19 Tea set designed by Henry Cole. V&A: 2741-4-1901.\*

2 Caricatures of Felix Summerly's 'Art Manufactures': wood engravings in Punch, vol.13(1847),p.227.

21 Herbert Minton: miniature by John Simpson, ?1848. Minton Archives, Stokeon-Trent.

22 Thomas Milner Gibson: watercolour by Charles Allen Du Val, 1843. National Portrait Gallery.

23 Journal of design (April 1850), vol. 3, double-page spread, unnumbered pages after p.64.

24 Lord Granville: wood engraving in Illustrated London News (1 March 1862), p.215.

Evenings at Home, oil painting by George Smith, 1852-3. Private Collection.

26 No.I, The Terrace, Kensington High Street. Royal Borough of Kensington and Chelsea Libraries and Arts Service.

27 Cole aged 37: miniature by John Simpson, 1846. The Cole family.\*

28 'Society of Arts' House': wood engraving in John Hollingshead, A concise history of the International Exhibition of 1862 (London, 1862), p.9.\*

Charles Wentworth Dilke: wood engraving in Illustrated London News (18 October 1851),p.509.\*

Colonel William Reid: wood engraving in Illustrated London News (27 September 1851),p.393.\*

3Colonel Charles Phipps: wood engraving in Illustrated London News (11 April 1863), p. 400."

32Henry Cole in 1851: pencil sketch by Prosper Lafaye, for an uncompleted paint-V&A: PDP, E.137-1968.\*

33Richard Redgrave: detail of a self-portrait in oil, c.1850. National Portrait Gallery.

34 John Charles Robinson: detail of a miniature by William Maw Egley, 1852. Private Collection."

3 5 Lyon Playfair: detail of a photograph by Maull & Polyblank, 1855. National Portrait Gallery.

Francis Fowke: detail of a photograph, c.1860. Private Collection.\*

37 Norman MacLeod, 25th Chief of MacLeod: detail o a portrait in ol by James Archer. In the collection of MacLeod of MacLeod, Dunvegan Castle. By permission of the Scottish National Portrait Gallery. Photograph: Antonia Reeve.

38 Philip Cunliffe-Owen: detail from a caricature, a colour lithograph, by 'Spy' in Vanity Fair (23 November 1878).\*

39 'Shere. Cottage of Henry Cole and Fir Hill, 186o': etching by Henry Cole, in his folder entitled Unpublished Amateur Etchings, By Henry Cole, C.B., 1860 to 1866. V&A: PDP,26470.3.\*

4'Mary in a bedroom at Shere, 1856': photograph by Henry Cole. V&A: PDP, Ph.835.77-1987.\*

4 1 Bird's-Eye View of the Paris Exhibition, 1867: wood engraving from Eugene Rimmel, Recollections of the Paris Exhibition of 1867 (London: Chapman and Hall, [1867]).\*

42 The Royal Horticultural Society's gardens and the Royal Albert Hall: anonymous tinted lithograph, c.1871. V&A: PDP, E.3-1952.\*

43 'Lecture on Cookery at the International Exhibition': wood engraving in Illustrated London News (17 May 1873), P.457.

44 Christmas card satirising Henry Cole's activities: watercolour by Frank Fowke, 1877.V&A:PDP, E.941-1976.\*

45 Cole and family at Hunstanton, 1881: photograph. Private Collection.

46 'King Cole': caricature, colour lithograph, by J. J. Tissot, in Vanity Fair (19 August 1871).\*

47 Henry Cole: pen drawing by Richard Doyle, c.1865. Department of Prints and Drawings, The British Museum.

48 Henry Cole, 1883: photograph of J. McNeill Whistler's unfinished and now destroyed oil portrait, inserted in vol. 1 (front flyleaf) of E. R. and J. Pennell, The

Life of James McNeill Whistler (London: emann, 1908, Alan Cole's copy). V&A: NAL (Special Collections).

## Figures

I Illustrations of public records: wood engravings after sketches by Cole, in his article, 'Public Records of Éngland', Penny Magazine (7 Aug 1841), pp.308-9.

2 Cole at New Year's party, 1830, disguised as a woman: pen and ink sketch by an anonymous artist. The Cole family.#

3From Henry Cole, Railway Travelling Chart, London &Brighton, 2nd ed. (London: Railway Chronicle Office, 1848). V&A: NAL, Special Collections.\*

4 Wood engraved cover or frontispiece design for Felix Summerly, A Handbook for .. Hampton Court, 2nd ed. (London: Bell, 1843). V&A: NAL, 236.D.26.\*

5 Pages from Felix Summerly, A glance at the Temple Church, 3rd ed. (London: Bell, 1844). V&A: NAL, 55.CC.1.\*

3 First page of catalogue, Art Manufactures. Collected by Felix Summerly, 4th ed., [1847], from a set of catalogues bound up for Cole, with the title page Catalogue of Felix Summerly's Art Manufactures. MDCCCXLVII and MDCCXLVIII (London, 1865). V&A: NAL, Special Collections.\*

7 Journal of design (December 1850), vol. 4, pp.118-119.\*

8 'Stancliffe, Darley Dale, August 1868': pen drawing by Henry Cole. V&A: PDP, E.3709-1934 in album 94.F.17.\*

9 The East London Museum: cartoon from Fun (6 July 1872), in the Cole Collection Press Cutting Book covering 1864-79. V&A: NAL, 55.CC.32, vol.1, P.57.\*

![](images/ee187d1d903b9a4f3b63e72afc1b628e73fc32d55e33dd22c3e4adb478b87956.jpg)

ElIZAbeTH BonytHoN is a nineteenth-century specialist whose connection with the V&A began with the Victorian Church Art exhibition in 197o-71. She is now an Honorary Fellow of the V&A Research Department. She has produced a typed transcript and index of the fifty-seven volumes of Cole's diaries, which means that she has a more intimate acquaintance with Cole than any other contemporary scholar. She has written articles on Cole, and the V&A published her King Cole: A picture portrait of Sir Henry Cole in 1982.

AnthonY Burton worked at the V&A in several capacities, including as Assistant Keeper of the National Art Library (1968-79), and Head of the Bethnal Green Museum of Childhood (1981-97). He worked on over a hundred exhibitions including Victorian Church Art. Until retirement in 2002 he was Senior Research Fellow in Museology and Museum History in the V&A Research Department. In 1999 he published Vision  Accident: The Story of the Victoria and Albert Museum, the first comprehensive history of the V&A's politics and personalities.

## Also available from V&A Publications:

A Grand Design: The Art of the Victoria and Albert Museum

Malcolm Baker and Brenda Richardson, eds

Vision & Accident: The Story of the Victoria & Albert Museum Anthony Burton
